what_it_does: Stores metadata for a channel attachment that links an image asset in the design-assets channel.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no body content
References an image via the URI image://design-assets/Visual Assets - Dev Space - Frame 8
Records an added timestamp in ISO 8601 with timezone
id is att-39209c41 and channel is design-assets
standouts: The target URI names a specific design frame: 'Visual Assets - Dev Space - Frame 8'
The file is purely a metadata record with no descriptive prose
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a fixed set of fields: id, channel, attachment_type, target, added
Uses an image:// URI scheme for asset targets
Separates frontmatter from an empty body with '---'
concerns: The target URI contains unescaped spaces, which may cause parsing or resolution issues
No title, caption, uploader, or asset dimensions are recorded
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that points to a named design image in the design-assets channel.
questions: Who added this attachment and for what purpose?
Does the image:// URI resolve to an actual stored file?
How does this frame relate to other frames in the 'Visual Assets - Dev Space' set?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file's purpose is to record an image attachment, and it does so via metadata fields.
intention_signals: To register a specific design image asset in the channel memory so it can be referenced and retrieved in context.
novelty: Uses an image:// URI with a human-readable, spaced asset name as the canonical target rather than a file path or hash.
contribution: Provides a concrete attachment record that maps an identifier to a design image asset in the design-assets channel, including a target URI and creation timestamp for retrieval and provenance.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter attachment record in the design-assets channel that points to the image asset 'Visual Assets - Dev Space - Frame 8' via an image:// URI and records its addition timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to catalog and make referenceable a specific design image within the channel memory ledger.
